name: Leafmap (Interactive Geospatial Visualization)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Qiusheng Wu / University of Tennessee

auth.notes: Local library; cloud storage auth via fsspec when reading cloud data

performance.notes: Browser-based rendering; tileserver for rasters; interactive via ipyleaflet

agent_readiness.notes: One-stop geospatial visualization in Jupyter. import leafmap. m = leafmap.Map(). m.add_basemap('OpenTopoMap'). m.add_shp('data.shp'). m.add_geojson('data.geojson'). m.add_raster('dem.tif', colormap='terrain'). m.add_cog_layer(url). m.add_stac_layer(url, collection, item). m.add_labels(gdf, 'column'). m.split_map(left_layer, right_layer). m.to_html('map.html'). COG/STAC support. Google Earth Engine optional (geemap). Many built-in basemaps.

gotchas: Best in Jupyter notebooks — limited value as standalone script
Google Earth Engine features require separate geemap + GEE auth
API is wide but shallow — complex customization requires ipyleaflet
Large rasters need COG format for browser-friendly serving
best_when: Quick interactive geospatial exploration in Jupyter
COG/STAC raster visualization without local data download
avoid_when: Non-Jupyter contexts (use folium for standalone HTML)
summary: Leafmap provides one-stop interactive geospatial visualization for Jupyter. m = leafmap.Map(). m.add_raster('dem.tif'). m.add_cog_layer(url). m.add_stac_layer(). m.split_map(). Many basemaps. HTML export. MIT by Qiusheng Wu. Best for quick Jupyter-based exploration of rasters, vectors, COG/STAC data. Not for production web apps — use folium or deck.gl. GEE features require geemap.
